revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:天磐船]]


'''Amano Iwafune''' (天磐船) is a vessel mentioned in [[Japanese mythology]] as the ship used by {{ill|Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi|lt=Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja|饒速日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ニギハヤヒ}}, a deity from [[Takamagahara|Takama-ga-hara]], to descend to [[Ashihara no Nakatsukuni|Nakatsukuni]] (Earth). 
== Overview ==
'''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'''
* {{ill|Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja|塩土老翁|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} told {{ill|Emperor Jimmu|en|Emperor Jimmu|qid=Q200188|lt=Emperor Jimmu|1=Emperor Jimmu}}, "To the east lies a fine land, surrounded by green mountains. Someone descended there aboard the Amano Iwafune." Emperor Jimmu believed that the individual was Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. (Emperor Jimmu Pre-Ascension, Year Kōin){{Sfn|Sakamoto|Ienaga|Inoue|Ōno|1994|p=200|qq=}}
* {{ill|Nagatsuhiko|ja|長髄彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagatsuhiko|qq=}} sent an envoy to Emperor Jimmu, stating, "Long ago, the child of the heavenly deities descended aboard the Amano Iwafune. His name is Kushi-tama Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto. He married my sister, {{ill|Mikashikiya-hime|ja|三炊屋媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mikashikiya-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, and they had a child named Kamimashitano-mikoto. I am serving under Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto." (Emperor Jimmu Pre-Ascension, Year Tsuchinoe-Uma, December){{Sfn|Sakamoto|Ienaga|Inoue|Ōno|1994|p=232|qq=}}
'''{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|qid=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}'''
* Amano Hiko Taka-nobe Mikoto, the child of {{ill|Ame-no-oshihomimi|ja|正哉吾勝々速日天押穂耳尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-oshihomimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノオシホミミ|1=Ame-no-oshihomimi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q462555}} and {{ill|Takuhadachiji-hime|ja|万幡豊秋津師比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takuhadachiji-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 栲幡千千姫命}}, descended to {{ill|Kawachi Province|en|Kawachi Province|qid=Q1047150|lt=Kawachi Province|1=Kawachi Province}} at Ikaruga-no-Take aboard the Amano Iwafune. He then moved to {{ill|Torimi Shiraniwayama|ja|鳥見白庭山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Torimi Shiraniwayama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} in the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}. While flying, he looked down and proclaimed, "Sorami-tsu Yamato no Kuni (The Void-Seen Land of Yamato)." (Heavenly Deities Chronicle, Heavenly Grandchild Chronicle){{Sfn|Keizai Zasshi Sha|1898|pp=215-216,251|qq=}}
{{Needs citation range|According to the shrine history of {{ill|Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja|石切劔箭神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishikiri Tsurugiya Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石切剣箭神社}}, the Amano Iwafune fleet included multiple ships rather than a single vessel.|date=May 2024|qq=}}
Later, "Yamato" became associated with the {{ill|Yamato Province|en|Yamato Province|qid=Q907495|lt=Yamato Province|1=Yamato Province}}, and "Japan" (Nihon) became the nation's official name after the {{ill|Taika Reform|en|Taika Reform|qid=Q570140|lt=Taika Reforms|1=Taika Reform}}. The Amano Iwafune, said to have carried Nigihayahi-no-Mikoto, is now enshrined at {{ill|Iwane Shrine (Katano)|ja|磐船神社 (交野市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwane Shrine (Katano)|1=Iwafune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11587926}} in {{ill|Katano|en|Katano|qid=Q819646|lt=Katano, Osaka|1=Katano}} and at {{ill|Iwane Grand Shrine|ja|磐船大神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwane Grand Shrine|qq=}} in {{ill|Kanan, Osaka|en|Kanan, Osaka|qid=Q2305508|lt=Kanan Town, Osaka|1=Kanan, Osaka}}, where visitors can view the sacred vessels. During the Taishō period, {{ill|Hikō Shrine|ja|飛行神社|simple|Hiko Shrine|az|Hiko məbədi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hikō Shrine|qq=}} was established in {{ill|Yawata|en|Yawata|qid=Q260923|lt=Yawata City, Kyoto|1=Yawata}}, inspired by these legends.
== The Stone Ship of Settsu Province ==
'''{{ill|Fudoki of Settsu Province|ja|摂津国風土記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fudoki of Settsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}'''
* When {{ill|Ame no Wakahiko|ja|天稚彦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame no Wakahiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to アメノワカヒコ}} descended from the heavens, {{ill|Ame-Sagume|ja|天探女|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-Sagume|1=Ame-no-Sagume|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10939684}} arrived aboard a stone ship at what is now called {{ill|Takatsu|en|Takatsu|qid=Q11671775|lt=Kōzu, Osaka|1=Takatsu}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1024920/1/30 |website=ndl.go.jp |access-date=May 10, 2024|qq=}}</ref>
'''{{ill|Chōya Gunsai|ja|朝野群載|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chōya Gunsai|1=Chōya Gunsai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11517735}}'''
* East of Settsu Province, at {{ill|Mihara Town|ja|味原町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mihara Town|1=Ajihara-cho|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q72929283}}, lies a stone ship. In ancient times, {{ill|Shitateruhime|ja|下照姫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shitateruhime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シタテルヒメ}} manifested there. The stone ship measures over 40 {{ill|hiro|ja|尋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/hiro|1=Hiro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11458676}} in length and 20 hiro in width, featuring depressions and a central sacred jewel called a {{ill|wish-fulfilling jewel|ja|如意珠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wish-fulfilling jewel|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. Facing northeast, the ship holds a shrine dedicated to stone deities.<ref>{{Cite web |title=National Diet Library Digital Collection |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/3436498/1/259 |website=ndl.go.jp |access-date=May 10, 2024|qq=}}</ref>
== References ==
{{Reflist|qq=}}
== Bibliography ==
* {{Cite book|title=National Historical Collection|date=August 6, 1898|year=1898|publisher=Keizai Zasshi Sha|ref=harv|author=Keizai Zasshi Sha|volume=7|doi=10.11501/991097|qq=}}
* {{Cite book|title=Nihon Shoki|date=September 16, 1994|year=1994a|publisher=Iwanami Shoten|ref=harv|last=Sakamoto|author=Sakamoto Tarō|first=Tarō|series=Iwanami Bunko|isbn=4-00-300041-2|volume=1|last2=Ienaga|author2=Ienaga Saburō|first2=Saburō|last3=Inoue|author3=Inoue Mitsusada|first3=Mitsusada|last4=Ōno|author4=Ōno Susumu|first4=Susumu|qq=}}

== See Also ==
* {{ill|List of mythological vehicles|ja|神話・伝説の乗り物の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of mythological vehicles|1=list of mythological vehicles|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q56353140}}
* {{ill|Ame-no-torifune|ja|鳥之石楠船神|fr|Ame-no-torifune|Ame-no-torifune=Ame-no-torifune|lt_ja=Bird Boat of Heavenly Pterocarya|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-torifune|qq=}}
* {{ill|Yada Kurushitama Hikojinja|ja|矢田坐久志玉比古神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yada Kurushitama Hikojinja|1=Yata ni Imasu Kushitamahiko Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11584205}}
* {{ill|Stone Boat Shrine (Murakami)|ja|石船神社 (村上市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Boat Shrine (Murakami)|1=Iwafune Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q124496786}}
* {{ill|Funayama Shrine|ja|船山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Funayama Shrine|1=Funayama Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119929665}}
